source_title: Big Data y periodismo en la sociedad red
author: Carlos Elías

extracts.extract: Digital journalism establishes the primacy of immediacy over reflection and interpretation and, very especially, to the detriment of quality.
extracts.chapter: Future of journalism profession in the network society

extracts.extract: If journalism wants to survive, it will have to take in young people and offer them good working conditions so that they can train in peace. But also, if it wants to survive, it cannot do without veteran journalists, who are the only ones capable of transmitting to the young the idiosyncrasies of the craft.
extracts.chapter: Tension between expert bloggers and craft journalism

extracts.extract: An economist --- or a physicist or a sociologist --- may be able to write a good economic --- or scientific --- analysis that is comprehensible to the general public, but it would be altogether ridiculous for them to publish a report in which they interview colleagues in the hallway or question themselves. It would be very difficult for them to write a vibrant chronicle of how a budget is debated in a parliament or how a new exoplanet is discovered.
extracts.chapter: Tension between expert bloggers and craft journalism

extracts.extract: Journalistic genres --- especially the chronicle and the feature report --- are far more attractive for achieving large audiences or goals of persuasion of public opinion than simple analyses or opinion columns by experts.
extracts.chapter: Future of journalism profession in the network society

extracts.extract: The best weapon of mass persuasion is beauty; and journalists have always tried to use art --- whether literary or visual art through the image --- not only to recount and publish the truth, but to bring it closer to the segment of the public most reluctant to become aware of real problems.
extracts.chapter: Tension between expert bloggers and craft journalism

extracts.extract: One would need to analyze why someone, motu proprio and without pay, begins to publish information.
extracts.chapter: Future of journalism profession in the network society

extracts.extract: A physicist or an economist working at a university does not contemplate denouncing corruption, uncovering a Watergate or a GAL scandal; rather, they will use the media to proselytize their point of view and sell more books, have more cited articles, or attract more funding for their research. It is not necessarily a social service, any more than the magazine published by an energy multinational or a tobacco company need be. Communication and journalism must not be confused.
extracts.chapter: Tension between expert bloggers and craft journalism
